search.c(3) Library Functions Manual search.c(3)

NAME

search.c - Interactive search filtering implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct log_search_state_t

Macros


#define MAX_LOG_SEARCH_PATTERNS 32
Maximum number of patterns to support. #define LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE 256
Input buffer size.

Functions


asciichat_error_t log_search_init (void)
Initialize interactive grep subsystem. void log_search_destroy (void)
Clean up interactive grep subsystem. void log_search_enter_mode (void)
Enter search mode (user pressed '/'). void log_search_exit_mode (bool accept)
Exit search mode. bool log_search_is_entering_atomic (void)
Check if grep is in entering mode (async-signal-safe). void log_search_signal_cancel (void)
Cancel grep mode from a signal handler (async-signal-safe). bool log_search_check_signal_cancel (void)
Check and clear the signal-cancel flag. bool log_search_is_entering (void)
Check if currently in input mode (typing pattern). bool log_search_is_active (void)
Check if filtering is active. bool log_search_should_handle (int key)
Check if a key should be handled by grep module. asciichat_error_t log_search_handle_key (keyboard_key_t key)
Process keyboard input for grep. asciichat_error_t log_search_gather_and_filter_logs (session_log_entry_t **out_entries, size_t *out_count)
Gather and filter logs for display. void log_search_render_input_line (int width)
Render grep input line at bottom of screen. bool log_search_get_match_info (const char *message, size_t *out_match_start, size_t *out_match_len)
Get match info for highlighting a log message. bool log_search_needs_rerender (void)
Check if screen needs immediate re-render. bool log_search_get_global_highlight (void)
Check if global highlighting (/g flag) is enabled in current pattern. void * log_search_get_pattern_singleton (void)
Get the compiled regex pattern for interactive grep (internal use). void * log_search_get_mutex (void)
Get the mutex protecting grep state. int log_search_get_input_len (void)
Get current input buffer length (must hold mutex). int log_search_get_cursor_position (void)
Get current cursor position in input buffer (must hold mutex). const char * log_search_get_input_buffer (void)
Get current input buffer content (must hold mutex). bool log_search_get_case_insensitive (void)
Check if case-insensitive flag is set (must hold mutex).

Detailed Description

Interactive search filtering implementation.

Implements vim-style / search functionality for terminal screens. Supports full /pattern/flags syntax with real-time filtering.

Definition in file search.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE 256

Input buffer size.

Function Documentation

bool log_search_check_signal_cancel (void )

Check and clear the signal-cancel flag.

Returns

true if grep was cancelled by a signal since last check

Called by the status screen loop to detect signal-initiated cancellation.

Definition at line 385 of file search.c.

385                                           {
386   bool expected = true;
387   return atomic_cas_bool(&g_search_state.signal_cancelled, &expected, false);
388 }




References atomic_cas_bool().






void
  log_search_destroy (void )


Clean up interactive grep subsystem. Frees all allocated
    resources. Safe to call multiple times.


Definition at line 197 of file search.c.


197                               {
198   if (!lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log_search_lifecycle)) {
199     return; // Already shut down or never initialized
200   }
201 
202   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
203 
204   // Free active patterns
205   for (int i = 0; i < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; i++) {
206     if (g_search_state.active_patterns[i]) {
207       // Note: pcre2_singleton handles its own reference counting
208       g_search_state.active_patterns[i] = NULL;
209     }
210   }
211 
212   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
213 }






References lifecycle_shutdown(), mutex_lock, and
    mutex_unlock.






void
  log_search_enter_mode (void )


Enter search mode (user pressed '/'). Saves current CLI --grep
    patterns, activates input mode, and prepares for interactive pattern
  entry.


Definition at line 219 of file search.c.


219                                  {
220   // Ensure cursor is visible for user typing
221   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
222 
223   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
224 
225   // Save current patterns (CLI --grep patterns)
226   asciichat_error_t result = grep_save_patterns();
227   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
228     log_warn("Failed to save filter patterns");
229   }
230 
231   // Clear input buffer
232   memset(g_search_state.input_buffer, 0, sizeof(g_search_state.input_buffer));
233   g_search_state.len = 0;
234   g_search_state.cursor = 0;
235 
236   // Pre-populate with CLI --grep pattern if available (only once at startup)
237   if (!g_search_state.cli_pattern_auto_populated) {
238     const char *cli_pattern = grep_get_last_pattern();
239     if (cli_pattern && cli_pattern[0] != '\0') {
240       // Skip leading slash if present (prompt already shows "/")
241       const char *pattern_to_use = cli_pattern;
242       if (cli_pattern[0] == '/') {
243         pattern_to_use = cli_pattern + 1;
244       }
245 
246       size_t pattern_len = strlen(pattern_to_use);
247       if (pattern_len < sizeof(g_search_state.input_buffer) - 1) {
248         memcpy(g_search_state.input_buffer, pattern_to_use, pattern_len + 1);
249         g_search_state.len = pattern_len;
250         g_search_state.cursor = pattern_len;
251         g_search_state.cli_pattern_auto_populated = true;
252 
253         // Compile and apply the pattern immediately so it starts filtering
254         grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
255         if (parsed.valid) {
256           // Store parsed flags
257           g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
258           g_search_state.fixed_string = parsed.is_fixed_string;
259           g_search_state.global_highlight = parsed.global_flag;
260           g_search_state.invert_match = parsed.invert;
261           g_search_state.context_before = parsed.context_before;
262           g_search_state.context_after = parsed.context_after;
263 
264           // Compile pattern if not fixed string
265           if (strlen(parsed.pattern) > 0 && !parsed.is_fixed_string) {
266             pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
267             if (singleton) {
268               pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
269               if (code) {
270                 g_search_state.active_patterns[0] = singleton;
271                 g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 1;
272               } else {
273                 g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
274               }
275             } else {
276               g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
277             }
278           }
279         }
280       }
281     }
282   }
283 
284   // Enter input mode
285   g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING;
286   atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING);
287   atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
288 
289   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
290 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_int(), grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_get_last_pattern(),
    grep_parse_pattern(), grep_save_patterns(),
    grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string,
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING, log_warn, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pcre2_options, terminal_cursor_show(),
    and grep_parse_result_t::valid.


Referenced by log_search_handle_key(),
    ui_status_display(), and ui_status_display_interactive().






void
  log_search_exit_mode (bool accept)


Exit search mode.


Parameters


accept If true, compile and activate typed
  pattern; if false, restore previous patterns


If accepting: Parses pattern with /pattern/flags syntax, compiles
    with PCRE2, and activates filtering. If canceling: Restores CLI --grep
    patterns.


Definition at line 292 of file search.c.


292                                        {
293   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
294 
295   if (g_search_state.mode != LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING) {
296     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
297     return;
298   }
299 
300   if (!accept) {
301     // Cancel - restore previous patterns and reset CLI pattern flag so user can re-populate
302     asciichat_error_t result = grep_restore_patterns();
303     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
304       log_warn("Failed to restore filter patterns");
305     }
306 
307     // Clear interactive grep patterns
308     for (int i = 0; i < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; i++) {
309       g_search_state.active_patterns[i] = NULL;
310     }
311     g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
312 
313     // Reset the CLI pattern auto-populated flag so user can re-enter grep mode with the pattern again
314     g_search_state.cli_pattern_auto_populated = false;
315 
316     g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE;
317     atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE);
318     atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
319     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
320     return;
321   }
322 
323   // Accept - parse and compile pattern
324   grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
325 
326   if (!parsed.valid) {
327     log_error("Invalid pattern format");
328     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
329     return; // Stay in input mode
330   }
331 
332   // Clear old regex patterns
333   for (int i = 0; i < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; i++) {
334     g_search_state.active_patterns[i] = NULL;
335   }
336   g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
337 
338   // Store parsed flags first
339   g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
340   g_search_state.fixed_string = parsed.is_fixed_string;
341   g_search_state.global_highlight = parsed.global_flag;
342   g_search_state.invert_match = parsed.invert;
343   g_search_state.context_before = parsed.context_before;
344   g_search_state.context_after = parsed.context_after;
345 
346   // Compile and store new pattern (if not fixed string)
347   if (strlen(parsed.pattern) > 0 && !parsed.is_fixed_string) {
348     pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
349     if (singleton) {
350       // Verify the pattern actually compiles
351       pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
352       if (code) {
353         g_search_state.active_patterns[0] = singleton;
354         g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 1;
355       } else {
356         // Compilation failed - fall back to fixed string matching
357         g_search_state.fixed_string = true; // Override to use fixed string instead
358       }
359     } else {
360       // Malloc failed - fall back to fixed string
361       g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
362     }
363   }
364   // For fixed strings, pattern stays in input_buffer and fixed_string=true
365 
366   g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE;
367   atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE);
368   atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
369 
370   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
371 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_int(), grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_parse_pattern(),
    grep_restore_patterns(), grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string, log_error,
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING,
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE, log_warn, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pcre2_options, and
    grep_parse_result_t::valid.


Referenced by log_search_handle_key(), and
    ui_status_display_interactive().






asciichat_error_t log_search_gather_and_filter_logs
  (session_log_entry_t ** out_entries, size_t * out_count)


Gather and filter logs for display.


Parameters


out_entries Output array of filtered log entries
  (caller must free with SAFE_FREE)


out_count Output count of filtered entries


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Hybrid approach:



  
1.

  
If --log-file specified: tail last N KB, parse entries

  
2.

  
Fetch entries from in-memory session_log_buffer (up to 100
    entries)

  
3.

  
Merge and deduplicate by timestamp

  
4.

  
Filter combined set with active PCRE2 patterns

  
5.

  
Return filtered entries (caller must free)




If no log file or read error: Falls back to in-memory buffer
  only.


Definition at line 535 of file search.c.


535                                                                                                           {
536   if (!out_entries || !out_count) {
537     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "out_entries and out_count must not be NULL");
538   }
539 
540   // Get logs from in-memory buffer
541   session_log_entry_t *buffer_entries =
542       SAFE_MALLOC(SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE * sizeof(session_log_entry_t), session_log_entry_t *);
543   if (!buffer_entries) {
544     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate log buffer");
545   }
546 
547   session_log_buffer_t *log_buf = log_get_session_log_buffer();
548   size_t buffer_count = session_log_buffer_get_recent(log_buf, buffer_entries, SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE);
549 
550   // Check if filtering is active (either regex patterns or fixed string)
551   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
552   bool has_regex_patterns = (g_search_state.active_pattern_count > 0);
553   bool has_fixed_string = (g_search_state.fixed_string && g_search_state.len > 0);
554   bool filtering_active = has_regex_patterns || has_fixed_string;
555 
556   if (!filtering_active) {
557     // No filtering active - return all entries
558     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
559     *out_entries = buffer_entries;
560     *out_count = buffer_count;
561     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
562   }
563 
564   // Filter entries with PCRE2
565   session_log_entry_t *filtered =
566       SAFE_MALLOC(SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE * sizeof(session_log_entry_t), session_log_entry_t *);
567   if (!filtered) {
568     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
569     SAFE_FREE(buffer_entries);
570     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate filtered buffer");
571   }
572 
573   size_t filtered_count = 0;
574 
575   // Create match data for PCRE2 matching
576   pcre2_match_data *match_data = NULL;
577   for (int p = 0; p < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; p++) {
578     if (g_search_state.active_patterns[p]) {
579       pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(g_search_state.active_patterns[p]);
580       if (code) {
581         match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(code, NULL);
582         break;
583       }
584     }
585   }
586 
587   if (!match_data && g_search_state.active_pattern_count > 0) {
588     // Couldn't create match data - fall back to returning all entries
589     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
590     SAFE_FREE(filtered);
591     *out_entries = buffer_entries;
592     *out_count = buffer_count;
593     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
594   }
595 
596   // Parse pattern once before loop (for fixed string mode)
597   grep_parse_result_t parsed = {0};
598   if (has_fixed_string) {
599     parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
600   }
601 
602   for (size_t i = 0; i < buffer_count; i++) {
603     const char *original_message = buffer_entries[i].message;
604     bool matches = false;
605 
606     // Strip ANSI codes to match against plain text (consistent with highlighting)
607     char plain_message[SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX] = {0};
608     const char *strip_ansi_codes_fn(const char *src, char *dst, size_t dst_size);
609     // Use inline stripping to avoid function call overhead
610     {
611       size_t pos = 0;
612       const char *src = original_message;
613       char *dst = plain_message;
614       size_t dst_size = sizeof(plain_message);
615 
616       while (*src && pos < dst_size - 1) {
617         if (*src == '\x1b' && src[1] != '\0' && src[1] == '[') {
618           // CSI sequence - skip until final byte
619           src += 2;
620           while (*src && pos < dst_size - 1 && !(*src >= 0x40 && *src <= 0x7E)) {
621             src++;
622           }
623           if (*src)
624             src++; // Skip final byte
625         } else {
626           dst[pos++] = *src++;
627         }
628       }
629       dst[pos] = '\0';
630     }
631 
632     size_t message_len = strlen(plain_message);
633 
634     // Fixed string matching
635     if (has_fixed_string) {
636       const char *search_pattern = parsed.pattern;
637 
638       if (g_search_state.case_insensitive) {
639         // Unicode-aware case-insensitive fixed string search
640         matches = (utf8_strcasestr(plain_message, search_pattern) != NULL);
641       } else {
642         // Case-sensitive fixed string search
643         matches = (strstr(plain_message, search_pattern) != NULL);
644       }
645     }
646     // Regex pattern matching
647     else if (has_regex_patterns) {
648       // Check against all active patterns (OR logic)
649       for (int p = 0; p < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; p++) {
650         pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = g_search_state.active_patterns[p];
651         if (!singleton) {
652           continue;
653         }
654 
655         pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
656         if (!code) {
657           continue;
658         }
659 
660         // Match against plain message (without ANSI codes)
661         int rc = pcre2_jit_match(code, (PCRE2_SPTR)plain_message, message_len, 0, 0, match_data, NULL);
662         if (rc >= 0) {
663           matches = true;
664           break;
665         }
666       }
667     }
668 
669     // Apply invert logic
670     if (g_search_state.invert_match) {
671       matches = !matches;
672     }
673 
674     if (matches) {
675       filtered[filtered_count++] = buffer_entries[i];
676     }
677   }
678 
679   // Free match data
680   if (match_data) {
681     pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
682   }
683 
684   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
685 
686   SAFE_FREE(buffer_entries);
687   *out_entries = filtered;
688   *out_count = filtered_count;
689   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
690 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_MEMORY, grep_parse_pattern(),
    log_get_session_log_buffer(), session_log_entry_t::message,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    session_log_buffer_get_recent(), SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE,
    SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX, SET_ERRNO, and
  utf8_strcasestr().

bool log_search_get_global_highlight (void )


Check if global highlighting (/g flag) is enabled in current
    pattern.


Returns


true if interactive grep is active and /g flag is set,
  false otherwise


Used by filter highlighting code to determine if all matches
    should be highlighted or just the first match. Returns false when
    interactive grep is inactive.


Definition at line 900 of file search.c.


900                                            {
901   // Use atomic read to avoid mutex contention with keyboard handler
902   int mode = atomic_load_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic);
903   if (mode == LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE) {
904     return false;
905   }
906 
907   // Safe read under mutex (quick operation)
908   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
909   bool result = g_search_state.global_highlight;
910   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
911 
912   return result;
913 }

  char * log_search_get_input_buffer (void )

bool log_search_get_match_info (const char * message,
  size_t * out_match_start, size_t * out_match_len)


Get match info for highlighting a log message.


Parameters


message Log message to check


out_match_start Output: start position of match in plain text


out_match_len Output: length of match


Returns


true if message matches current filter pattern, false
  otherwise


Used by display code to apply highlighting to matching portions of
    logs. Returns match position for use with log_filter_highlight().


Definition at line 733 of file search.c.


733                                                                                                     {
734   if (!message || !out_match_start || !out_match_len) {
735     return false;
736   }
737 
738   *out_match_start = 0;
739   *out_match_len = 0;
740 
741   // Use atomic read to avoid mutex contention with keyboard handler
742   int mode = atomic_load_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic);
743   if (mode == LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE) {
744     return false;
745   }
746 
747   // Make a safe copy of state under mutex, then release before doing expensive work
748   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
749 
750   bool has_fixed_string = g_search_state.fixed_string && g_search_state.len > 0;
751   bool case_insensitive = g_search_state.case_insensitive;
752   char pattern_copy[LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE];
753   size_t pattern_len = g_search_state.len;
754   int pattern_count = g_search_state.active_pattern_count;
755   pcre2_singleton_t *patterns_copy[MAX_LOG_SEARCH_PATTERNS];
756 
757   if (has_fixed_string) {
758     // Parse the input buffer to extract just the pattern part (without flags)
759     grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
760     const char *src = parsed.valid ? parsed.pattern : g_search_state.input_buffer;
761     SAFE_STRNCPY(pattern_copy, src, sizeof(pattern_copy) - 1);
762     pattern_copy[sizeof(pattern_copy) - 1] = '\0';
763     pattern_len = strlen(pattern_copy); // Update pattern_len for the actual parsed pattern
764   }
765 
766   for (int i = 0; i < pattern_count && i < MAX_LOG_SEARCH_PATTERNS; i++) {
767     patterns_copy[i] = g_search_state.active_patterns[i];
768   }
769 
770   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
771 
772   // Now do matching without holding mutex
773   size_t message_len = strlen(message);
774   bool matches = false;
775 
776   // Fixed string matching
777   if (has_fixed_string) {
778     const char *found = NULL;
779 
780     if (case_insensitive) {
781       found = utf8_strcasestr(message, pattern_copy);
782     } else {
783       found = strstr(message, pattern_copy);
784     }
785 
786     if (found) {
787       matches = true;
788 
789       // Convert byte position to character position (UTF-8 aware)
790       size_t byte_pos = (size_t)(found - message);
791       size_t char_pos = 0;
792       for (size_t i = 0; i < byte_pos && message[i] != '\0';) {
793         uint32_t codepoint;
794         int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)(message + i), &codepoint);
795         if (utf8_len <= 0)
796           utf8_len = 1;
797         i += utf8_len;
798         char_pos++;
799       }
800 
801       // Count characters in the match (pattern_len is in bytes for the pattern)
802       size_t match_char_len = 0;
803       for (size_t i = 0; i < pattern_len && found[i] != '\0';) {
804         uint32_t codepoint;
805         int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)(found + i), &codepoint);
806         if (utf8_len <= 0)
807           utf8_len = 1;
808         i += utf8_len;
809         match_char_len++;
810       }
811 
812       *out_match_start = char_pos;
813       *out_match_len = match_char_len;
814     }
815   }
816   // Regex pattern matching
817   else if (pattern_count > 0) {
818     pcre2_match_data *match_data = NULL;
819     for (int p = 0; p < pattern_count; p++) {
820       if (patterns_copy[p]) {
821         pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(patterns_copy[p]);
822         if (code) {
823           match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(code, NULL);
824           break;
825         }
826       }
827     }
828 
829     if (match_data) {
830       for (int p = 0; p < pattern_count; p++) {
831         pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = patterns_copy[p];
832         if (!singleton) {
833           continue;
834         }
835 
836         pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
837         if (!code) {
838           continue;
839         }
840 
841         int rc = pcre2_jit_match(code, (PCRE2_SPTR)message, message_len, 0, 0, match_data, NULL);
842         if (rc >= 0) {
843           matches = true;
844           PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
845 
846           // Convert byte positions to character positions (UTF-8 aware)
847           size_t byte_start = (size_t)ovector[0];
848           size_t byte_end = (size_t)ovector[1];
849 
850           // Count characters up to byte_start
851           size_t char_start = 0;
852           for (size_t i = 0; i < byte_start && message[i] != '\0';) {
853             uint32_t codepoint;
854             int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)(message + i), &codepoint);
855             if (utf8_len <= 0)
856               utf8_len = 1;
857             i += utf8_len;
858             char_start++;
859           }
860 
861           // Count characters up to byte_end
862           size_t char_end = char_start;
863           for (size_t i = byte_start; i < byte_end && message[i] != '\0';) {
864             uint32_t codepoint;
865             int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)(message + i), &codepoint);
866             if (utf8_len <= 0)
867               utf8_len = 1;
868             i += utf8_len;
869             char_end++;
870           }
871 
872           *out_match_start = char_start;
873           *out_match_len = char_end - char_start;
874           break;
875         }
876       }
877       pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
878     }
879   }
880 
881   return matches;
882 }






References asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(),
    atomic_load_int(), grep_parse_pattern(),
    LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE,
    MAX_LOG_SEARCH_PATTERNS, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    grep_parse_result_t::pattern, pattern_count,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, utf8_decode(), utf8_strcasestr(), and
    grep_parse_result_t::valid.


Referenced by terminal_screen_render().






void
  * log_search_get_mutex (void )


Get the mutex protecting grep state.


Returns


Pointer to the grep state mutex (internal use only)


Used by terminal rendering to perform atomic read of grep input
    for consistent rendering without flicker.


Definition at line 940 of file search.c.


940                                  {
941   return (void *)&g_search_state.mutex;
942 }






Referenced by terminal_screen_render().






void
  * log_search_get_pattern_singleton (void )


Get the compiled regex pattern for interactive grep (internal
    use).


Returns


Opaque PCRE2 singleton for the current pattern, or NULL
  if inactive/no pattern


Internal function used by filter highlighting code to enable
    global match highlighting in interactive grep mode. Returns NULL when not in
    active mode or if pattern is fixed string type (no regex compilation).


Definition at line 915 of file search.c.


915                                              {
916   // Use atomic read to check if active
917   int mode = atomic_load_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic);
918   if (mode == LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE) {
919     return NULL;
920   }
921 
922   // Safe read under mutex
923   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
924 
925   // Return the compiled pattern if we have one (not fixed string)
926   void *result = NULL;
927   if (g_search_state.active_pattern_count > 0 && !g_search_state.fixed_string) {
928     result = (void *)g_search_state.active_patterns[0];
929   }
930 
931   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
932 
933   return result;
934 }






References atomic_load_int(),
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE, mutex_lock, and
  mutex_unlock.


Referenced by grep_highlight_colored().






asciichat_error_t log_search_handle_key (keyboard_key_t
  key)


Process keyboard input for grep.


Parameters


key The keyboard key to process


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Uses keyboard_read_line_interactive() to handle text editing.
    Supports backspace, delete, arrows, home, end, enter, escape, UTF-8.




Pattern validation happens live via validator callback. Enter
    compiles and activates pattern. Escape cancels and restores previous
    patterns.


Definition at line 419 of file search.c.


419                                                             {
420   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
421 
422   // If not in input mode, check if user pressed '/'
423   if (g_search_state.mode != LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING) {
424     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
425 
426     // Check for '/' key (key already passed as parameter, don't read again!)
427     if (key == '/') {
428       log_search_enter_mode();
429     }
430     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
431   }
432 
433   // In input mode - use keyboard_read_line_interactive()
434   // CRITICAL: Keep mutex held during keyboard_read_line_interactive() to prevent races
435   // with the rendering thread. The validator callback and buffer modifications must be
436   // synchronized with readers in log_search_gather_and_filter_logs().
437   keyboard_line_edit_opts_t opts = {
438       .buffer = g_search_state.input_buffer,
439       .max_len = sizeof(g_search_state.input_buffer),
440       .len = &g_search_state.len,
441       .cursor = &g_search_state.cursor,
442       .echo = false,                       // We handle rendering ourselves
443       .mask_char = 0,                      // No masking
444       .prefix = NULL,                      // Don't render prefix - log_search_render_input_line() handles it
445       .validator = validate_pcre2_pattern, // Live validation
446       .key = key                           // Pass the pre-read key
447   };
448 
449   keyboard_line_edit_result_t result = keyboard_read_line_interactive(&opts);
450 
451   switch (result) {
452   case LINE_EDIT_ACCEPTED:
453     // Unlock before exit_mode since it acquires the mutex
454     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
455     log_search_exit_mode(true); // Parse and accept pattern
456     break;
457   case LINE_EDIT_CANCELLED:
458     // Unlock before exit_mode since it acquires the mutex
459     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
460     log_search_exit_mode(false); // Restore previous
461     break;
462   case LINE_EDIT_CONTINUE:
463     // Still editing - compile pattern for live filtering
464     // Mutex is still held here
465 
466     // If buffer is empty, clear patterns to show all logs (don't exit grep mode)
467     if (g_search_state.len == 0) {
468       g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
469       mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
470       atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
471       break;
472     }
473 
474     // Parse and compile pattern for live filtering
475     grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
476 
477     if (!parsed.valid) {
478       // Invalid pattern - keep previous patterns active (don't clear)
479       mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
480       atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
481       break;
482     }
483 
484     // Valid pattern - update filtering state
485     if (parsed.is_fixed_string) {
486       // Fixed string matching - no regex compilation needed
487       g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
488       g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
489       g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
490     } else {
491       // Regex pattern - compile with full pcre2_options
492       pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
493       if (singleton) {
494         pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
495         if (code) {
496           g_search_state.active_patterns[0] = singleton;
497           g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 1;
498           g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
499           g_search_state.fixed_string = false;
500         } else {
501           // Compilation failed - fall back to fixed string matching
502           g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
503           g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
504           g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
505         }
506       } else {
507         g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
508         g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
509         g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
510       }
511     }
512 
513     // Store parsed flags
514     g_search_state.global_highlight = parsed.global_flag;
515     g_search_state.invert_match = parsed.invert;
516     g_search_state.context_before = parsed.context_before;
517     g_search_state.context_after = parsed.context_after;
518 
519     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
520     atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
521     break;
522   case LINE_EDIT_NO_INPUT:
523     // No input available
524     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
525     break;
526   }
527 
528   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
529 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), atomic_store_bool(),
    grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_parse_pattern(),
    grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string, log_search_enter_mode(),
    log_search_exit_mode(), LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pcre2_options, and
    grep_parse_result_t::valid.

    ui_status_display_interactive().






asciichat_error_t log_search_init (void )


Initialize interactive grep subsystem.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Must be called before using any other log_search functions.
    Initializes state, mutex, and loads CLI --grep patterns if any.


Definition at line 130 of file search.c.


130                                         {
131   // Use lifecycle API for unified initialization
132   if (!lifecycle_init_once(&g_log_search_lifecycle)) {
133     // Already initialized, acquire mutex lock and return
134     mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
135     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
136   }
137 
138   // Winner of init_once - initialize mutex and state
139   g_log_search_lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX;
140   g_log_search_lifecycle.sync.mutex = &g_search_state.mutex;
141   lifecycle_init(&g_log_search_lifecycle, "log_search");
142 
143   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
144 
145   // Initialize state (careful not to destroy the mutex!)
146   // Save the mutex before clearing state
147   mutex_t saved_mutex = g_search_state.mutex;
148   memset(&g_search_state, 0, sizeof(g_search_state));
149   // Restore the mutex
150   g_search_state.mutex = saved_mutex;
151 
152   // Check if there are CLI --grep patterns to use
153   const char *cli_pattern = grep_get_last_pattern();
154 
155   // Start in INACTIVE mode if no CLI pattern is provided.
156   // Only use patterns from CLI --grep arguments, not default patterns.
157   // This ensures logs are visible by default and only filtered if explicitly requested.
158   if (!cli_pattern || cli_pattern[0] == '\0') {
159     // No CLI pattern - start inactive
160     g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE;
161     atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE);
162     lifecycle_init_commit(&g_log_search_lifecycle);
163     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
164     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
165   }
166 
167   // Load CLI pattern into input buffer
168   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_search_state.input_buffer, cli_pattern, LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE - 1);
169   g_search_state.len = strlen(cli_pattern);
170   g_search_state.cursor = g_search_state.len;
171   g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE;
172   atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE);
173 
174   // Compile the initial pattern
175   grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(cli_pattern);
176   if (parsed.valid) {
177     pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
178     if (singleton) {
179       g_search_state.active_patterns[0] = singleton;
180       g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 1;
181     }
182     g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
183     g_search_state.fixed_string = parsed.is_fixed_string;
184     g_search_state.global_highlight = parsed.global_flag;
185     g_search_state.invert_match = parsed.invert;
186     g_search_state.context_before = parsed.context_before;
187     g_search_state.context_after = parsed.context_after;
188   }
189 
190   atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
191   lifecycle_init_commit(&g_log_search_lifecycle);
192 
193   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
194   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
195 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_int(), grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_get_last_pattern(),
    grep_parse_pattern(), grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string, lifecycle_init(),
    lifecycle_init_commit(), lifecycle_init_once(),
    LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX, LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE,
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,

Check if grep is in entering mode (async-signal-safe).


Returns


true if in GREP_MODE_ENTERING


Uses atomic load only (no mutex). Safe to call from signal

bool log_search_needs_rerender (void )


Check if screen needs immediate re-render.


Returns


true if pattern changed and re-render needed, false
  otherwise


Uses atomic flag for lock-free checking in render loops.
    Automatically clears flag after returning true.


Definition at line 888 of file search.c.


888                                      {
889   bool needs = atomic_load_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender);
890   if (needs) {
891     atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, false);
892   }
893   return needs;
894 }

void
  log_search_render_input_line (int width)


Render grep input line at bottom of screen.


Parameters


width Terminal width in columns


Renders: /<pattern>/flags█ with cursor position
    indicated. Cursor shown as block character or with background color.




If pattern is invalid (validator returns false), shows red
    background.


Definition at line 696 of file search.c.


696                                              {
697   (void)width; // Reserved for future use (line truncation)
698 
699   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
700 
701   if (g_search_state.mode != LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING) {
702     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
703     return;
704   }
705 
706   // Render the pattern and position terminal cursor at current edit position
707   char output_buf[512];
708 
709   // Build output: "/" + pattern
710   int pattern_len = snprintf(output_buf, sizeof(output_buf), "/%.*s", (int)g_search_state.len, g_search_state.input_buffer);
711 
712   if (pattern_len > 0 && pattern_len < (int)sizeof(output_buf)) {
713     // Write the pattern to terminal
714     platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, output_buf, pattern_len);
715 
716     // After writing, cursor is at: column = 1 (for "/") + len + 1
717     // We want it at: column = 1 (for "/") + cursor_position + 1
718     // So we need to move back by: len - cursor_position columns
719     int cursor_offset = (int)g_search_state.len - (int)g_search_state.cursor;
720     if (cursor_offset > 0) {
721       // Move left by the difference
722       terminal_move_cursor_relative(-cursor_offset);
723     }
724   }
725 
726   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
727 }






References LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, platform_write_all(), and
    terminal_move_cursor_relative().


Referenced by terminal_screen_render().






bool log_search_should_handle (int key)


Check if a key should be handled by grep module.


Parameters


key Keyboard key code


Returns


true if grep should handle this key, false to pass
  through


Returns true for:


    
  
  • 
  
  • All keys when in GREP_MODE_ENTERING (to edit pattern)
    • 



Definition at line 404 of file search.c.


404                                        {
405   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
406 
407   // If in input mode, handle all keys
408   if (g_search_state.mode == LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING) {
409     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
410     return true;
411   }
412 
413   // If not in input mode, only handle '/' to enter mode
414   bool should_handle = (key == '/');
415   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
416   return should_handle;
417 }






References LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING, mutex_lock, and
    mutex_unlock.

void
  log_search_signal_cancel (void )


Cancel grep mode from a signal handler (async-signal-safe). Sets
    an atomic flag that the status screen loop checks on its next iteration.
    Does not use mutexes or allocate memory.
